timeline.description: The Election Commission of India announces the election schedule, including dates for nominations, voting, and results.
details: Model Code of Conduct comes into effect immediately
Government cannot announce new policies or schemes
Election Commission sets up monitoring mechanisms
Dates for each phase are officially notified

timeline.description: Candidates file their nomination papers with the Returning Officer of their constituency.
details: Candidates submit Form 2A/2B with required documents
Security deposit of ₹25,000 (General) or ₹12,500 (SC/ST) required
Affidavit declaring criminal cases, assets, and education mandatory
Scrutiny of nominations follows within a few days

timeline.description: Last date for candidates to withdraw their nominations. Final list of contesting candidates is published.
details: Candidates may withdraw for strategic or personal reasons
Final list published after withdrawal deadline
Election symbols allotted to candidates
Campaign intensifies after final list

timeline.description: Political parties and candidates campaign to win voter support through rallies, advertisements, and outreach.
details: Door-to-door canvassing and public rallies
TV, radio, print, and social media advertising
Campaign must stop 48 hours before polling (silence period)
Expenditure limits apply to all candidates
No appeals to religion, caste, or communal feelings allowed

timeline.description: Voters cast their votes at designated polling stations using Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs).
details: Voting hours typically 7 AM to 6 PM
Voter must carry valid photo ID (EPIC or approved alternatives)
Indelible ink applied on left index finger
EVMs with VVPAT ensure transparency
Booth-level officers manage polling stations
Special arrangements for elderly and disabled voters

timeline.description: Votes are counted at designated counting centers under strict supervision.
details: Postal ballots counted first
EVM votes counted round-by-round
VVPAT slips verified for random 5 machines per constituency
Counting agents from all candidates present
Results declared constituency by constituency

timeline.description: The Election Commission officially declares the results and the winning candidates.
details: Results published on ECI website in real-time
Winning candidate gets certificate of election
Government formation process begins
Election petitions can be filed within 45 days

guides.title: Voter Registration
guides.description: How to register as a voter and get your Voter ID (EPIC) card

steps.title: Check Eligibility
steps.content: You must be an Indian citizen, at least 18 years old on the qualifying date (January 1st of the year of revision of electoral roll), of sound mind, and not disqualified under any law.

steps.title: Gather Documents
steps.content: Keep ready: Proof of age (birth certificate, school certificate, passport), Proof of address (utility bills, bank passbook, ration card), and passport-size photographs.

steps.title: Fill Form 6
steps.content: Fill Form 6 (Application for inclusion in Electoral Roll) online at voters.eci.gov.in or the Voter Helpline App. You can also get a physical form from the nearest Electoral Registration Office.

steps.title: Submit Application
steps.content: Submit the form online with uploaded documents or physically at the Electoral Registration Office. Keep your application reference number safe.

steps.title: Verification Visit
steps.content: A Booth Level Officer (BLO) will visit your address for verification. Ensure someone is home and keep your original documents ready for verification.

steps.title: Receive Voter ID
steps.content: Once verified and approved, your name will be added to the electoral roll. You will receive your EPIC (Voter ID) card. You can also download the e-EPIC from the Voter Helpline App.

guides.title: How to Vote
guides.description: Step-by-step guide for casting your vote on election day

steps.title: Find Your Polling Station
steps.content: Check your polling station details on the Voter Helpline App or voters.eci.gov.in. Your polling station is assigned based on your registered address.

steps.title: Carry Valid ID
steps.content: Bring your EPIC (Voter ID) card or any of the 12 approved photo IDs: Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, PAN Card, MNREGA Job Card, Bank Passbook with photo, etc.

steps.title: Arrive at Polling Station
steps.content: Visit your assigned polling station during voting hours (typically 7 AM - 6 PM). Follow the queue and wait for your turn.

steps.title: Identity Verification
steps.content: The polling officer will verify your identity, check your name in the voter list, and apply indelible ink on your left index finger.

steps.title: Cast Your Vote
steps.content: Enter the voting compartment, find your preferred candidate/party on the EVM ballot unit, and press the blue button next to their name and symbol. Check the VVPAT slip to confirm your choice.

steps.title: Exit and Collect Slip
steps.content: After voting, exit the polling booth. You may receive a voter slip as proof of having voted. Your vote is secret and protected by law.

guides.title: Postal Ballot Voting
guides.description: How to vote via postal ballot if you're eligible

steps.title: Check Eligibility
steps.content: Postal ballot is available for: Service voters (armed forces, government employees posted away), Voters on election duty, Senior citizens (80+), Persons with disabilities, COVID-affected voters, and essential service workers.

steps.title: Apply for Postal Ballot
steps.content: Submit Form 12 to the Returning Officer of your constituency. The application must be submitted within the prescribed time after notification of election.

steps.title: Receive Ballot Paper
steps.content: The Returning Officer will dispatch the postal ballot paper to your registered address along with instructions and a declaration form.

steps.title: Mark Your Vote
steps.content: Mark your vote on the ballot paper in privacy. Place it in the inner cover, then in the outer cover along with the signed declaration.

steps.title: Return the Ballot
steps.content: Post the sealed cover back to the Returning Officer. It must reach before the counting date to be valid.

guides.title: Filing Election Complaints
guides.description: How to report election violations and file complaints

steps.title: Identify the Violation
steps.content: Common violations include: voter bribery, booth capturing, hate speech, misuse of government machinery, exceeding expenditure limits, and campaign during silence period.

steps.title: Use cVIGIL App
steps.content: Download the cVIGIL app by ECI. Take a photo or video of the violation with GPS location. Submit it through the app for immediate action.

steps.title: Contact Election Commission
steps.content: Call the ECI helpline at 1950, email complaints@eci.gov.in, or visit the National Grievance Services portal at ngsp.in.

steps.title: Track Your Complaint
steps.content: Every complaint gets a unique tracking ID. You can track the status through the cVIGIL app or the ECI website. Flying squads respond within minutes.

faqs.question: What is the minimum voting age in India?
faqs.answer: 18 years. You must be at least 18 years old on the qualifying date (January 1st of the year of revision of electoral roll).

faqs.question: What is NOTA?
faqs.answer: NOTA stands for 'None Of The Above'. It is an option on the EVM that allows voters to reject all candidates. However, even if NOTA gets the most votes, the candidate with the next highest votes wins.

faqs.question: What is the Model Code of Conduct?
faqs.answer: The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) is a set of guidelines issued by the Election Commission for political parties and candidates to follow during elections. It covers general conduct, meetings, processions, polling day behavior, and use of government machinery.

faqs.question: Can I vote if I don't have my Voter ID card?
faqs.answer: Yes! You can use any of the 12 approved alternative photo IDs including Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, PAN Card, MNREGA Job Card, or Bank Passbook with photo.

faqs.question: What is EVM and VVPAT?
faqs.answer: EVM (Electronic Voting Machine) is the device used for casting votes electronically. VVPAT (Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail) is attached to the EVM and prints a paper slip showing the candidate's name and symbol, allowing voters to verify their vote.

faqs.question: How are election results declared?
faqs.answer: After polling, EVMs are stored in strong rooms under CCTV. On counting day, postal ballots are counted first, followed by EVM votes. Results are declared round by round and published on the ECI website in real-time.

glossary.definition: Electors Photo Identity Card - the official voter ID card
Electronic Voting Machine used for casting votes
Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail for vote verification
Election Commission of India - the constitutional body conducting elections
Model Code of Conduct - guidelines for parties during elections
Booth Level Officer - the field-level election officer
None Of The Above - option to reject all candidates
Returning Officer - oversees election in a constituency
The lower house of Indian Parliament (House of the People)
